title: euclea kellau
intro: Euclea kellau, also known as the "Cassod tree", is a species of plant found in tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Euclea kellau include treating fever, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. The herbal preparations of Euclea kellau typically involve drying and crushing the leaves or roots, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a paste.
